question_text: A fossil fuel power station has an efficiency of 35% and burns 1000 tonnes of coal per day. If coal releases 25 MJ of energy per kilogram, how much thermal energy is wasted as heat each day?
explanation: Total energy from coal: 1000 tonnes = 1,000,000 kg × 25 MJ/kg = 25,000,000 MJ. Useful energy: 35% × 25,000,000 = 8,750,000 MJ. Wasted heat: 65% × 25,000,000 = 16,250,000 MJ. (Note: Check calculation—wasted should be 65% of 25×10⁶ MJ = 16.25×10⁶ MJ = 16,250,000 MJ or 1.625×10¹⁰ J)
